title: Speedboat Hire in Estepona: 2026 Costs & Tips
html:
Estepona, on Spain's Costa del Sol, is a prime base for speedboat hire, with departures from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona offering direct access to the Mediterranean. Expect to pay from 250 EUR for a half-day rental. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators. Compare verified boat listings and book instantly through their platform; boat hire Estepona options start from around 250 EUR for a 4.5-metre vessel, rising to 600 EUR for a premium six-hour charter.

Where to set off in Estepona

Most speedboat hires depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, a modern marina with ample berths and easy parking. Alternatively, launch from the adjacent Playa de la Rada for trailer-launched boats. Popular destinations include the coves of Bahía de Estepona, the exclusive Marina del Sol, and the nearby port of La Duquesa to the east. The tidal range is minimal, so access is straightforward year-round. You can boat hire Estepona for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

  1. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, ideal for thrill-seekers; seats 6; from 350 EUR half-day. See the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing for details.
  2. Rib tender, perfect for coastal exploration and watersports; capacity 4; from 200 EUR half-day.
  3. Bowrider, great for families and sunbathing; seats 5, 6; from 280 EUR half-day.
  4. Axopar 22, versatile and fast; seats 8; from 400 EUR half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona?

The cost to rent a boat in Estepona varies by type, duration, and season, with prices starting at 200 EUR for small motorboats.

  • Shared tour: from 60 EUR per person, 2 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 200, 350 EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 1,200, 2,000 EUR for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht (full-day): from 2,500 EUR for luxury experience

High season (July, August 2026) sees a 20% premium. Midweek bookings often reduce costs. All prices are in EUR and include basic safety equipment.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Estepona?

You need a valid boating licence to rent a speedboat in Spain, but some operators offer licenced skippers for hire.

Spanish law requires a *Licencia de Navegación* (for boats up to 6 metres) or a *Patrón de Navegación Básica* (for larger craft). For licenced boats, some owners provide a skipper at extra cost. Always confirm licence requirements with the operator before booking.

In Estepona, the local *Levante* wind can pick up in the afternoon, so book morning slots for the calmest conditions. Ask your operator about the day's forecast.

At a glance

  • Price range: 60, 2,500 EUR
  • Peak months: July and August 2026
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours
  • Max capacity on speedboat: 6 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona

Can I rent a speedboat without a licence in Estepona?

Yes, if you hire a boat with a licenced skipper included, or choose a vessel under 6 metres that requires a simple licence you can obtain locally.

What is the best time of year for speedboat hire in Estepona?

The best weather is from May to September, with July and August being hottest and busiest; spring and autumn offer quieter conditions and lower prices.

How far can I go from Estepona in a half-day rental?

With a half-day (4 hours) you can easily reach Marbella (10 nautical miles east) or Gibraltar (15 nm west), including time for swimming stops.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona or list your own.
